secondary_sdks.context: Historical name — renamed to @adcp/sdk at 5.23.0 (parallel-shipped through 5.25.1). EOL at 5.25.1 as a separate package. Migration to @adcp/sdk@^7 completed in PR #257 (zod 3→4 alongside, since SDK 7.x peer-deps zod^4.1.5). Watcher keeps this entry to catch any unexpected republish (e.g. a security patch backport to the EOL line).

tracked_issues.context: Q3 systemic FAIL→SKIP applicability bug, half-shipped 2026-04-30. Phase 1 (runner gate): adcp-client#1063 merged into @adcp/sdk 5.25.0+; runner now honors Storyboard.required_tools and emits clean skip_reason:'missing_tool' when no required tool is advertised. We're on 5.25.1 — already have it. Phase 2 (storyboard annotations, the remaining maintainer task): past_start_enforcement + peer media-buy storyboards still need required_tools:[create_media_buy,update_media_buy] in their YAML. Doesn't affect us — we're applicability-gated out of those storyboards via supported_protocols:[signals] before the tool gate fires. Status downgraded from needs-wg-review (bokelley, 2026-05-01); milestone still 3.1.0. Drop after phase 2 lands.

Filed 2026-04-29, closed 2026-04-30. Fix shipped in @adcp/sdk 5.25.0; we're on 5.25.1. Tracking kept transiently because the adjacent spec-side normative note ('Storyboards MUST report SKIP, not FAIL, when supported_protocols excludes the scenario's required protocol') was filed as a small follow-up — closure event will fire when that lands. Drop after spec follow-up resolves.

Filed 2026-05-03. Runner bug: signal_owned/activate_on_agent storyboard step omits required `destinations` and `idempotency_key` per /schemas/3.0.1/signals/activate-signal-request.json. Schema-conformant agents have no signal of intent (agent vs platform). Workshop-insurance defensive heuristic shipped in this repo (correlation_id parsing in src/mcp/server.ts:callActivateSignal — search 'REMOVE WHEN adcp#4009 ships'). When the storyboard fix ships and Addie's evaluator confirms badge issuance, REVERT the heuristic — restore the simpler 2-stage destinationType precedence.

Filed 2026-05-07 via Addie. RFC 9421 webhook signing migration coordination. DEFERRED — blocked on #3064 (docs default to RFC 9421) + #3360 (grade tooling fails on HMAC-only). @bokelley reframed: 'the installed base of live buyer agents is ~empty right now' — it's an on-ramp problem, not migration. Original 80% threshold was meaningless against ~zero population. Right metric: 'no new HMAC implementers after date X', enforced via four on-ramp surfaces (docs / grade tooling / SDK ✓done / storyboards-untracked). Storyboard surface is the only piece without a tracker; we self-pledged to take it (see #4205 comment 4409426562). Resurfaces when #3064 + #3360 land. Discussion artifact: docs/DISCUSSION_SIGNAL_PAYMENTS.md v5.

Filed 2026-05-07 via Addie (Track A.0 JWKS adoption baseline gates signal-payment chain-of-custody work). DEFERRED — blocked on #4205. Same on-ramp reframe as #4205 applies: original 30% threshold was meaningless against ~zero population. Empirical adoption snapshot retained as named-partner inventory baseline for #3360, NOT as a WG-vote target. Resurfaces when #4205 resolves. Companion to docs/DISCUSSION_SIGNAL_PAYMENTS.md v5 + docs/SPEC_PROPOSAL_REVOKE_ACTIVATION.md.

Filed 2026-05-09 by @bokelley as the 4.0-milestoned structural follow-up after #4271 closed #4270. Drops `required: ["authentication"]` from reporting-webhook.json AND from the artifact_webhook block in create-media-buy-request.json (an extra surface beyond our original inventory, surfaced by upstream protocol-expert review during #4271). Schema descriptions already advertise the 4.0 behavior. Labels: claude-triaged, schema, needs-wg-review. Milestone: 4.0. Tracks our 9421-default opt-in path for both push-notification AND reporting webhooks — folds into trip-wire 3 of local issue #248 (item-3 migration). Drop after the 4.0 release lands or this issue is closed.

Filed 2026-05-16 by @bokelley as the umbrella framing for signal epistemic-model RFCs (#4472 PBA audience_model, #4475 structured market identifiers, @lszczesiak's ID-graph follow-up). Three cross-cutting decisions: D1 shared `{system, value, version?}` primitive vs per-dimension types; D2 vocabulary policy (adopt / carry by reference / accept fragmentation); D3 declarative-only vs attestation-capable. We posted implementer comment 4468154221 voting D1 shared primitive + offered to take a strawman draft-PR. Process proposal posted as comment 4468168781 — strawman by 2026-05-19, WG vote by 5/22, D1 in 3.1.x patch line by 5/28 (3.1.0 beta milestone due 5/29), row-level RFCs proceed in 4.0 against the decided shape, D2/D3 deferred to research-tier. Strawman PR #4622 filed 2026-05-16 (draft, discussion-only). 2026-05-16 round 1 convergence: @lszczesiak (=@lukasz-pubx) posted D1 vote on umbrella + reviewed strawman — agreed shared primitive, two refinements accepted in revision 5a0ebfe7: (1) rename `id`→`value` (cross-axis neutrality); (2) add `converted` to fidelity enum + new `system-reference-conversion.json` `{from, to, method, method_details?}` factoring "how preserved?" (enum) separately from "via what?" (structure). 2026-05-16 round 2 (revision ac4c6183): Lukasz follow-up — method enum extended 4→7 (`id_graph | name_match | crosswalk | upscaled | inferred | projected | custom`); `method_provider?` added as optional vendor-identity field (LiveRamp / ID5 / IAB / Nielsen / Comscore, opaque-by-convention, not closed enum). 2026-05-17 round 3 (revision 9716bb15): @bokelley issuecomment-4470049814 'Measurement is the third sibling' — epistemic-model table maps 1:1 onto delivery measurement, not just signals. D1 leverage doubles. Round 3 promotes measurement methodology to first-class in schema descriptions + adds measurement examples (nielsen_p_18_49 currency, measurement_source:set_top_box). No structural change. 2026-05-17 customer-set shift: #4475 (one of three signals-side D1 customers) closed by @bokelley with rationale 'we have geo_metro + geo_postal_areas already, extend those vs new concept' — D1's value prop sharpens to 'stop dimension-schema proliferation BEFORE it starts on newly-modeled rows' (ID graphs, measurement methodology, taxonomy versioning, signals-agent federation) vs 'replace per-dimension schemas that already work'. Remaining D1 customers: #4472 / #2041 / @lszczesiak ID-graph RFC / signals-agent federation (us + Affinity Answers, added to AAO 5/17). PR #4622 body updated 5/18 + umbrella comment posted (issuecomment-4478114164). 2026-05-16 @lukasz-pubx APPROVED PR #4622 (formal +1 sitting); awaiting @bokelley triage. Drop when D1 decision lands OR maintainer triages 'won't do for 3.1.x'.

Measurement source attribution on delivery reports — OPEN. Filed pre-#4616 but reframed by @bokelley issuecomment-4470049814 (2026-05-17) as the measurement-side instance of D1 in the epistemic-model umbrella. If D1 lands as shared `{system, value, version?}` primitive, #2041's `source_type` field should be a `SystemReference` (e.g. `{system: "measurement_source", value: "set_top_box"}`) NOT a flat enum; methodology blend (`is_deduplicated`, weighting mix) becomes a `conversion` block on the row. Adoption happens in #2041's own PR once D1 settles. Companion to signals row-RFCs #4472 / #4475. Drop when #2041 closes or migrates to a different shape.

Already-merged (2026-04 timeframe) — `completion_source: seller_attested | vendor_attested` qualifier on delivery measurement rows. Referenced in @bokelley's #4616 third-sibling framing as the shipped prior art for decision D3 (verifiability / attestation). Pattern: a structured field naming WHO attested, layered on top of the declarative content. The strawman has room to add `attestation?: {attester: SystemReference, ...}` as a sibling field on consuming schemas without breaking — extension hook to design into D1 from the start. Tracking transiently so we don't lose the precedent reference when D3 sequences. Drop when D3 sequencing decision is made (likely 4.0).

external_registries.name: agenticadvertising_org
external_registries.context: AAO official agent registry (https://agenticadvertising.org/registry/). Fires when agents are added, removed, or rename. Identity = agent URL (stable across renames; surfaces show the new name in the diff). Primary value: catch new ecosystem entrants without manual checks. As of 2026-05-17 the registry has 18 entries (was 21 on 2026-05-04; churn includes the Celtra trailing-slash dedup on 5/16). Type distribution: ~13 sales, 1 creative (Celtra), 1 buying (Swivel), 2 signals (us + Affinity Answers, added 2026-05-17). Affinity Answers (https://mcp.affinityanswers.com/mcp) is a pure-catalog signals provider — exposes only get_adcp_capabilities + get_signals, no activate_signal; data_provider_domains=["affinityanswers.com"]; FastMCP-based; advertises a ui://signals/view.html MCP UI resource. Concrete D1 motivator: federation across two signals-only agents (us + AA) needs consistent reference-frame primitives, which is exactly what #4616's {system, value, version?} primitive provides. Federation candidate worth probing further if cross-agent signal search becomes a workshop priority.
